FEE PD FOR JOINT STIPULATION TO EXTEND TIME FOR DEFT'S TO FILE A SPECIAL MTN TO STRIKE; (PROPOSED) ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT GRIFFITHS, GRANT AN INDIVIDUAL STORM SUSTAINABILITY, LTD, AN AUSTRALIAN LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GRANT GRIFFITHS, AND, STORM SUSTAINABILITY, LTD, MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF ALL CLAIMS BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS AND DISMISS OR STAY THE ACTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STAY ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION GRANTED AS TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND TO ONLY AS TO AN ASPECT OF THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION (3RD CAUSE OF ACTION). THE 1ST, 2ND, AND 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (EXCEPT FOR THE ONE ASPECT GOING TO ARBITRATION) ARE STAYED UNTIL ARBITRATION IS COMPLETED, AND REMAIN IN THIS COURT. COUNSEL WILL MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE WORDING FOR THE PROPOSED ORDER REGARDING THE ASPECT OF THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION THAT WILL GO TO ARBITRATION. (COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Of All Claims Brought By Pltfs And Dismiss Or Stay The Action, Or In The Alternative, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration;
Off Calendar Jun-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS GRANT GRIFFITHS, and, STORM SUSTAINABILITY, LTD, Motion to Compel Arbitration of All Claims Brought By Plaintiffs and Dismiss or Stay the Action, or in the Alternative, to Stay Action Pending Arbitration. Appearances: Stephen S. Smith, of Greenberg Glusker (310) 553-3610 appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Paul M. Zieff, Esq. Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, (415) 956-2828 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel arbitration granted as to the 4th and 5th causes of action, and to only as to an aspect of the Fraud cause of action (3rd cause of action). The 1st, 2nd, and 3rd cause of action (except for the one aspect going to arbitration) are stayed until arbitration is completed, and remain in this Court. Counsel will meet and confer as to the wording for the Proposed Order regarding the aspect of the Fraud cause of action that will go to arbitration. (Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
